name: dh-autoreconf
title: dh-autoreconf
summary: debhelper add-on to call autoreconf and clean up after the build
description:
debhelper add-on to call autoreconf and clean up after the build
dh-autoreconf provides a debhelper sequence addon named 'autoreconf' and two
commands, dh_autoreconf and dh_autoreconf_clean.
.
The dh_autoreconf command creates a list of the files and their checksums,
calls autoreconf and then creates a second list for the new files.
.
The dh_autoreconf_clean command compares these two lists and removes all
files which have been added or changed (files may be excluded if needed).

commands.name: dh_autoreconf
commands.description: Call autoreconf -f -i and keep track of the changed files.

arguments.description: Enable support for -Wl,--as-needed in all ltmain.sh files identical to the one of the
arguments.raw: --as-needed  Enable support for -Wl,--as-needed in all ltmain.sh files identical to the one of the

commands.name: dh_autoreconf_clean
commands.description: Clean all changes made by dh_autoreconf

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dh_autoreconf because debhelper add-on to call autoreconf and clean up after the build.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dh_autoreconf as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dh_autoreconf [debhelper options] [-Xitem] [--mode=mode] [program -- params]` as documented upstream for dh_autoreconf.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dh_autoreconf with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dh_autoreconf --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
